*Generated by Claude*

# Viecz Presentation Strategy Debate — 2026-04-18

6 Opus agents debated from philosophy, architecture, marketing, UX, user advocacy, and devil's advocate perspectives to define the project philosophy and perfect the 5-minute HCMUS I&E Round 2 presentation.

**Context:** HCMUS I&E 2025 competition, Round 2 (So khao). 5-minute slide presentation. Judges have already read submission docs. Previous simulated audit: 3 NO, 1 Conditional YES.

---

## Project Philosophy

> Students will help each other for small pay if finding each other takes 30 seconds instead of 30 minutes. Viecz is the fastest way to test that belief.

**Underlying principles (unstated, structure everything):**
- **Proximity creates obligation** — the 500 people within 500m of you are your latent support network
- **Campus as commons** — the map isn't a feature, it's a worldview
- **Dignity of micro-work** — paying 50k VND for carrying furniture is more dignifying than owing a social debt
- **Trust infrastructure for the unconnected** — freshmen from provinces who aren't in any Zalo group have zero access to the informal economy; Viecz serves them

**Key reframe:** Viecz doesn't compete with Zalo groups. It serves the moments Zalo groups fail — urgency, strangers, and money.

---

## Debate Participants

| Agent | Role | Key Contribution |
|---|---|---|
| Philosopher | Deep WHY, worldview | "Proximity creates obligation" framework; pivoted from explicit philosophy to falsifiable hypothesis |
| Architect | System design, tech framing | Proved live demo is unsafe (map tiles need internet); identified 3 architectural bets as presentation frame |
| Marketer | Narrative, positioning | Proposed Zalo screenshot hook, "pre-launch by design" framing, 8-slide structure |
| UX Designer | Visual strategy | Prioritized screens, proposed CSS fixes, device mockup frames, dark/light disconnect solution |
| User Advocate | Student perspective | Created Lan/Minh scenarios; identified food delivery as killer use case; "September freshman wave" as GTM |
| Devil's Advocate | Stress test | Identified fatal flaw (team composition); killed misleading claims; forced philosophy pivot |

---

## Five Disputes Resolved

### Dispute 1: Live Demo vs. Pre-recorded Video

| Agent | Vote |
|---|---|
| Philosopher | Pre-recorded + optional live |
| Architect | Pre-recorded (revised from live after analysis) |
| Marketer | Live demo + video backup |
| UX Designer | Screen recording, no live |
| User Advocate | Pre-loaded live + backup |
| Devil | Video with 4 conditions for live |

**Verdict: Pre-recorded 45-sec screen capture.** The architect's final analysis was decisive — MapTiler tiles have zero offline fallback, making live demo too risky for the hero visual. QR code on closing slide lets judges verify on their own phones.

**Architect's key finding:** Map tiles require `https://api.maptiler.com/maps/streets/style.json` with no service worker, no tile cache, no offline support. If venue WiFi drops, the map shows blank gray canvas. Cannot run locally either — MapTiler has no self-hosted option on free tier.

### Dispute 2: Opening Hook

**Unanimous: Lan's freshman story.** All 6 agreed.

> "Lan just moved into KTX. She needs help carrying a bookshelf to the 5th floor. She knows nobody. She posts in a Zalo group — 200 members, zero replies. Meanwhile, Minh on the 3rd floor was 30 meters away the whole time. The help existed. What didn't exist was a way to connect them."

The philosopher conceded philosophy should be implicit. The devil insisted QA members must be able to tell the story too.

### Dispute 3: Pre-launch Framing

**Unanimous: Don't spin it.** One sentence: "The product is complete. Our pilot starts June 2026." If pressed: "We prioritized getting escrow right before handling real money."

Do NOT say "pre-launch by design." Do NOT elaborate or apologize. Forward momentum, not retrospective justification.

### Dispute 4: Philosophy Placement

**Consensus: Near the end, embedded, never explicit.**

- Philosophy is implicit in the opening story (the word choices: "help each other," "nearby," "find each other")
- Explicit statement appears only at the close: "We don't create demand for help — we make the help that already exists visible."
- Philosopher's breakthrough: replaced the philosophy statement with a falsifiable hypothesis as the ask: "Give us 50 students and one semester — we'll prove whether this works."

### Dispute 5: Slide Count

| Agent | Vote |
|---|---|
| Philosopher | 8-9 |
| Architect | 8 |
| Marketer | 9 |
| UX Designer | 10 |
| User Advocate | 10 |
| Devil | 10 |

**Verdict: 9 slides** (median).

---

## Final 9-Slide Presentation Structure

| # | Slide | Time | Presenter Says | Slide Shows |
|---|-------|------|----------------|-------------|
| 1 | Cover | 5s | "Xin chao, chung toi la doi Viecz" | Name + tagline |
| 2 | Problem | 45s | Lan's story: freshman, bookshelf, KTX, zero Zalo replies. Ends: "The help existed. What didn't exist was a way to connect them." | Zalo screenshot (real or realistic mockup) |
| 3 | How it works | 30s | 4-step flow: post, map, accept, pay | Flow diagram |
| 4 | MAP HERO | 40s | "We didn't add a map to a task list. We built a task list on top of a map. Every task has GPS coordinates because urgency needs proximity." | Full-width map, HCMUS campus, annotated pins |
| 5 | Demo video | 45s | Voiceover: walk through map, task, chat, escrow | 45-sec screen recording (embedded MP4) |
| 6 | Why different | 45s | 3 points: proximity-first, micro-escrow from 5k VND, $8/month cost | 3 glass cards rewritten |
| 7 | Pilot plan | 30s | "Pilot starts June 2026 at HCMUS. 50 users, 200 transactions." | KPIs + timeline |
| 8 | Team | 20s | "One architect, three QA. Recruiting a growth lead — that's what mentorship is for." | Team cards (reframed) |
| 9 | Close | 15s | "We don't create demand for help — we make the help that already exists visible. Scan and try it now." | QR code (big, centered) + URL |

**Total: ~4:35, 25-second buffer.**

---

## Prepared Q&A Answers

| Question | Answer |
|---|---|
| How many real transactions? | "We haven't run a formal pilot yet. The product is deployed and payment-ready. Pilot starts June." |
| Why pay 15% vs Zalo? | "You're not paying for posting. You're paying for finding someone in 10 minutes, knowing they'll show up, and escrow protection if they don't." |
| Is this a 1-person project? | "Yes, one person wrote the code. That's a signal — one dev shipped a full-stack platform in 5 months. Three QA validated every feature." |
| Safety/disputes? | "University email verification. Escrow auto-refunds if task isn't confirmed. Admin review within 24h." |
| TaskRabbit clone? | "TaskRabbit proved people pay strangers for tasks. We built for Vietnam: 5k VND transactions, bank transfer, no credit card needed." |
| Unit economics? | Have exact PayOS fee structure ready. Be honest if margin is thin — volume solves it. |

---

## Immediate Prep Tasks

1. Record 45-sec screen capture of happy path (map → task → chat → escrow)
2. Take new map screenshot zoomed into HCMUS campus (denser pins)
3. Add QR code to closing slide
4. CSS fixes: `box-shadow: 0 0 40px rgba(0,0,0,0.6)` on screenshots + bump max-height to 280px
5. Replace 0-balance wallet screenshot with PayOS checkout or remove
6. Drill all team members on Q&A answers — every person must answer every question

---

## Key Insights from the Debate

### What the philosopher contributed
- "Proximity creates obligation" — the deepest framing
- Pivoted from explicit philosophy to falsifiable hypothesis (stronger)
- "The campus is already a community of mutual aid — Viecz makes it visible"
- Conceded that philosophy belongs in word choices, not on slides

### What the architect contributed
- Three "architectural bets" framing: proximity (map), trust (escrow), accessibility ($8/month cost)
- Killed the live demo with concrete technical analysis (MapTiler has no offline fallback)
- TaskRabbit vs Viecz architectural comparison table
- Confirmed production site has 200 seeded tasks across 35 Vietnamese locations

### What the marketer contributed
- "Pre-launch by design" framing (later modified to drop the phrase)
- Proposed cutting slides from 14 to 8-9
- "Show, don't tell" principle — live product in a room of mockups
- Grab analogy: "Grab localized Uber for SEA. We're localizing TaskRabbit for Vietnamese students."

### What the UX designer contributed
- MAP must be hero slide (biggest audit failure was not showing it)
- CSS fixes achievable in 1 hour (box-shadow, screenshot sizes)
- Device mockup frames to bridge dark/light disconnect
- Cut login, registration, profile screens — generic and not differentiating

### What the user advocate contributed
- Lan (freshman) and Minh (broke sophomore) scenarios — compelling characters
- Food delivery as killer use case (daily frequency beats everything)
- "September freshman wave" as natural acquisition moment
- "We don't compete with Zalo — we serve the moments Zalo fails"

### What the devil's advocate contributed
- Fatal flaw is TEAM COMPOSITION, not traction — if QA can't answer a question, it's over
- Five misleading claims in current slides (identified and corrected)
- "Pre-launch by design" is spin — forced honest alternative
- Insisted every team member must explain escrow, map, and business model
- Four conditions for live demo (moot after architect killed live demo)

---

## Claims Corrected (Devil's Audit)

| Original Claim | Problem | Corrected Version |
|---|---|---|
| "No platform in VN or SEA serves student micro-tasks" | Facebook Marketplace, Zalo groups exist informally | "No dedicated platform with escrow for Vietnamese student micro-tasks" |
| "Only 22.1% do part-time work" | 78% not working equally means they may not need this | "Of the 22.1% who work part-time, most are underserved — and the 78% may be deterred by friction of finding flexible work" |
| "Breakeven at 40 tx/month" | Only covers VPS, not real business costs | "Server cost recovery at 40 tx/month" (not breakeven) |
| ">70% test coverage" | Low for a system handling real money | Either raise to 95%+ for payment code, or stop highlighting as strength |
| "7/7 core features" | Meaningless without defining them and proving they work with real users | "7/7 features built and tested" (not "validated") |
